summary: Tested P1 add-ons: image upload + image messages + /files auth modes + Slack-style reactions toggle + WS realtime + in-tab notifications scaffolding. Backend 100% pass on all 9 checks. Frontend E2E: alice uploads PNG via attach-image-button → bob receives image instantly via WS → bob reacts 🔥 via popover → alice sees reaction chip with count in realtime. Toggle-off path also works. Notification permission requested on Chat mount (granted via context.grant_permissions).
passed: POST /api/upload with image/png + Bearer → 200 with {path, content_type, size}
POST /api/upload with text/plain → 400 'Only image files allowed'
POST /api/messages with image_path only (no text) → 201, message persisted with empty text + image_path
POST /api/messages with neither text nor image_path → 400 'Message text or image required'
GET /api/files/<path>?auth=<jwt> → 200, content-type image/png, exact stored bytes (12528)
POST /api/messages/{id}/reactions: alice 🔥 → {🔥:[alice]}; bob 🔥 → {🔥:[alice,bob]}; alice again → {🔥:[bob]}; bob again → {} (key removed)
Frontend: attach-image-button + hidden image-file-input present and wired
Frontend: image message renders with data-testid=message-image-<id> (PNG dice visible in screenshot)
Frontend: react-button-<id> appears, popover opens, 🔥 click adds reaction chip 'reaction-chip-<id>-🔥' with count
Realtime: bob's image arrival appears in alice's chat without refresh; bob's reaction chip syncs to alice without refresh (observed '🔥2' count)
Toggle off: clicking own chip decrements/removes user (count 2 → 1 observed)
notes: An older test message (with an invalid 1-byte PNG) still shows 'Image unavailable' placeholder — expected per agent note; not a bug.
In-tab Notification API verified only at the permission-request layer (Chat mounts and prompts). Actual Notification rendering is browser-driven and not asserted in script.
Two image bubbles were sent during the test (one from setup curl, one from the UI flow) — both rendered correctly.
